title: Seven Pebbles by Sunset
synopsis: When mysterious gifts start appearing on the pebble beach, little robot Orbit must solve the puzzle before sunset arrives with the summer storm. Each day brings a new clue, and Orbit discovers that sometimes the best mysteries are solved together.

pages.text: CLINK-CLANK-WHOOSH! The waves tossed something shiny onto the pebble beach. Orbit's amber eye telescoped out—click-click-CLICK—to get a closer look. What could it be? What could it be? The small robot's antenna fluttered with excitement as thunder rumbled far, far away. A bright yellow package, no bigger than a seashell, sat nestled between two smooth gray stones. Pop! Pop! Pop! Orbit's metal feet made happy sounds on the pebbles. But wait—dear reader, do you see what Orbit sees? There's something written on the package in swirly letters: "Gift One of Seven. Open by sunset, or the mystery stays forever!"

illustration.description: A tall thin bipedal exploration robot with a dented silver shell, solar panels for shoulders, a single amber telescopic eye, and a fluttering antenna bends down to examine a small yellow package on a pebble beach. The scene shows detailed hidden objects scattered among countless smooth stones: tiny shells, sea glass pieces, driftwood fragments, small crabs, colorful pebbles, seaweed tangles, and various beach treasures. Storm clouds gather in the distance while golden sunlight filters through.
illustration.alt: Orbit discovering the first mysterious gift on the cluttered pebble beach

pages.text: Rip-rip-RIP! The package opened with a satisfying sound. Inside was a tiny blue whistle and a note: "Blow three times when you need a friend." How curious! How strange! Orbit tucked the whistle safely in a compartment near his solar panels. The storm clouds rolled closer—RUMBLE-rumble-BOOM—and the sun seemed lower than before. Six more gifts to find! Six more puzzles to solve! But sunset was coming fast, fast, fast.

illustration.description: A tall thin bipedal exploration robot with a dented silver shell, solar panels for shoulders, a single amber telescopic eye, and a fluttering antenna holds a tiny blue whistle and examines a note. The pebble beach around him is filled with magnifying glass perspective details: intricate patterns in the stones, tiny periwinkle shells, bits of sea glass catching light, small pieces of driftwood carved smooth by waves, and various hidden beach treasures scattered throughout the detailed environment.
illustration.alt: Orbit reading the note from the first gift while examining the tiny whistle

pages.text: SPLASH-splash-SPLASH! The next morning brought gift number two—a red package tucked behind a piece of driftwood. Inside: a small compass that spun and spun and spun. "Point this toward your biggest wish," said the note. Orbit's antenna wiggled with wonder! Wednesday's gift was purple and contained a tiny telescope. "Look for what's coming," the note whispered. Thursday brought a green package with a small bell inside—TING-aling-TING! "Ring when hope feels far away."

illustration.description: A tall thin bipedal exploration robot with a dented silver shell, solar panels for shoulders, a single amber telescopic eye, and a fluttering antenna stands surrounded by opened gift packages scattered across the pebble beach. The detailed search and find scene shows the compass, telescope, and bell among countless beach objects: various sized pebbles in different colors, shells of many shapes, twisted driftwood pieces, sea glass, small crabs hiding, seaweed, and tiny treasures hidden throughout the busy organized composition.
illustration.alt: Orbit with multiple gifts discovered on the cluttered pebble beach

pages.text: But oh my! Oh dear! The storm was getting closer. CRACK-crackle-BOOM went the thunder. The waves grew bigger—WHOOSH-crash-WHOOSH! Orbit's eye blinked nervously. The sun was sliding down, down, down toward the horizon. Three more gifts to find, and time was running like water through his metal fingers! Well, if robots had fingers, which Orbit didn't, but you know what I mean, don't you?

illustration.description: A tall thin bipedal exploration robot with a dented silver shell, solar panels for shoulders, a single amber telescopic eye, and a fluttering antenna looks up at dramatic storm clouds while standing on a pebble beach. The detailed environment shows the organized chaos of a beach scene with hidden objects everywhere: shells partially buried in pebbles, driftwood pieces of various sizes, colorful stones, sea glass catching the dimming light, small creatures scurrying, and various beach treasures scattered in a cluttered but organized composition.
illustration.alt: Orbit looking anxiously at approaching storm clouds over the detailed beach

pages.text: BEEP-beep-BEEP! Orbit's sensors detected something orange half-buried near the water's edge. Gift number five! But this package was different—bigger and heavier. Inside was a small flag on a stick, bright as sunshine, bright as hope! The note said: "Plant this where everyone can see. Call your team—you're not meant to be alone." Team? Team? What team? Orbit looked around the empty beach. Just pebbles and waves and wind and... wait!

illustration.description: A tall thin bipedal exploration robot with a dented silver shell, solar panels for shoulders, a single amber telescopic eye, and a fluttering antenna holds a bright flag while standing near the water's edge of a pebble beach. The magnifying glass perspective shows incredible detail in the scene: water droplets on stones, tiny shells being moved by waves, sea foam bubbles, various pebbles of different patterns and colors, pieces of driftwood in different shapes, seaweed patterns, and countless small beach objects creating a busy but organized search and find environment.
illustration.alt: Orbit discovering the flag gift near the water with detailed beach objects

pages.text: The small robot remembered the whistle! Tweet-tweet-TWEET! He blew three times, just like the note said. Nothing happened. The wind howled—WOOOOSH! The waves crashed—SPLASH! Then... did you hear that? A faint beep-beep-beeping from somewhere behind the big rocks! Another robot? More robots? Orbit's antenna nearly vibrated off his head with excitement! But the sun was SO low now, painting the sky orange and pink. Sunset was coming fast!

illustration.description: A tall thin bipedal exploration robot with a dented silver shell, solar panels for shoulders, a single amber telescopic eye, and a fluttering antenna blows a small whistle while standing among large rocks on a pebble beach. The detailed hidden objects scene shows the busy environment filled with countless items to discover: different types of shells, varied pebble patterns, driftwood in many shapes, sea glass pieces, small crabs and creatures, seaweed formations, and various beach treasures scattered throughout the cluttered room-like beach scene.
illustration.alt: Orbit blowing the whistle among rocks with detailed beach treasures everywhere

pages.text: CLANK-whirr-BEEP! Three more robots appeared from behind the rocks! They were different shapes and sizes—one was round and bouncy, one was square and steady, one was tall and stretchy. But they all had the same excited beep-beep-BEEPING! "We've been waiting!" said Round Robot. "We got gifts too!" said Square Robot. "We're supposed to help!" said Stretchy Robot. Together, together, together they searched the beach—CLINK-clank-scatter-SEARCH!

illustration.description: A tall thin bipedal exploration robot with a dented silver shell, solar panels for shoulders, a single amber telescopic eye, and a fluttering antenna stands with three other robots of different shapes on a pebble beach. The detailed search and find composition shows the beach environment cluttered with discoverable objects: shells of every variety, pebbles in rainbow colors, twisted driftwood pieces, sea glass fragments, tiny creatures, seaweed patterns, and countless small treasures hidden throughout the organized but busy scene.
illustration.alt: Orbit meeting three other robots on the treasure-filled pebble beach

pages.text: Gift number six was silver and hidden under a piece of seaweed. Inside: a small puzzle piece shaped like a heart. "One piece of the whole," read the note. "Share with your team to make it complete." But where was the seventh gift? Where, where, WHERE? The robots spread out across the pebble beach—scatter-scatter-SEARCH! Round Robot rolled, Square Robot marched, Stretchy Robot reached high and low. The sun touched the horizon. Time was almost up!

illustration.description: A tall thin bipedal exploration robot with a dented silver shell, solar panels for shoulders, a single amber telescopic eye, and a fluttering antenna searches alongside three other robots across a pebble beach filled with incredible detail. The magnifying glass perspective reveals hundreds of small objects: shells, sea glass, pebbles of various patterns, driftwood pieces, small crabs, seaweed tangles, and countless tiny treasures creating a where's waldo style environment perfect for searching and finding.
illustration.alt: Four robots searching the incredibly detailed pebble beach for the final gift

pages.text: "FOUND IT!" beeped Square Robot. The seventh gift was golden and glowing, nestled right where they'd planted the bright flag together. Inside the final package were three more puzzle pieces and a note that made Orbit's circuits sing: "The greatest treasure isn't gold or gems—it's friends who help and never give up!" CLICK-click-SNAP! The four puzzle pieces fit together perfectly, making a beautiful picture of... four robots on a pebble beach!

illustration.description: A tall thin bipedal exploration robot with a dented silver shell, solar panels for shoulders, a single amber telescopic eye, and a fluttering antenna stands with three other robots around a completed puzzle on a pebble beach. The detailed hidden objects scene shows the beach environment packed with treasures: countless shells and pebbles, driftwood sculptures, sea glass catching golden light, small creatures, seaweed patterns, and hundreds of tiny discoveries scattered throughout the busy organized composition, all bathed in sunset colors.
illustration.alt: Four robots completing the puzzle together on the treasure-packed beach at sunset

pages.text: Just as the sun kissed the waves goodbye—SPLASH-sparkle-GLOW—the storm clouds rolled away! Tweet-TING-beep-HOORAY! The robots celebrated with all their sounds together. They'd solved the mystery! They'd found all seven gifts! But most importantly, dear reader, they'd found each other. And as the first stars twinkled hello, Orbit realized the best gift of all wasn't in any package—it was right there on the pebble beach, beeping and laughing and being together. The mystery was solved, but the friendship? That would last forever and ever and ever.

illustration.description: A tall thin bipedal exploration robot with a dented silver shell, solar panels for shoulders, a single amber telescopic eye, and a fluttering antenna celebrates with three other robots on a pebble beach under a star-filled sky. The detailed search and find environment shows the beach transformed by starlight and moonbeams, with countless discoverable objects: shells reflecting light, pebbles creating patterns, driftwood sculptures, sea glass sparkling, small nocturnal creatures, and hundreds of tiny treasures scattered throughout the magical nighttime beach scene.
illustration.alt: Four robots celebrating friendship under the stars on the detail-rich pebble beach

coverPrompt.description: A tall thin bipedal exploration robot with a dented silver shell, solar panels for shoulders, a single amber telescopic eye, and a fluttering antenna stands on a pebble beach scattered with colorful small objects. Dark storm clouds gather overhead while the golden sun sits low on the horizon. The beach is filled with detailed patterns of smooth stones, driftwood pieces, seashells, and seven mysterious wrapped gifts of different sizes.
coverPrompt.alt: Robot Orbit on a pebble beach with storm clouds and mysterious gifts

questions.question: What did Orbit have to do by sunset?
questions.explanation: Orbit had to find all seven mysterious gifts before sunset, or the mystery would stay forever!

questions.question: What did the blue whistle do when Orbit blew it?
questions.explanation: When Orbit blew the whistle three times, it called the other robots who came to help solve the mystery together.

questions.question: How many robots worked together to solve the mystery?
questions.explanation: Four robots worked together: Orbit, Round Robot, Square Robot, and Stretchy Robot!

questions.question: What was inside the final golden gift?
questions.explanation: The golden gift contained three puzzle pieces that completed the picture when combined with the piece from gift six.

questions.question: What did Orbit learn was the best gift of all?
questions.explanation: Orbit realized that friendship with the other robots was the best gift of all—better than any package!
